headline: Restaurant Introduces 'No Phone Zone' Where Using Your Phone Results In Public Shaming
summary: The 'Present Moment Cafe' has live shame officers who will announce your phone usage to the entire restaurant.
fullBody: The Present Moment Cafe has opened in downtown New York with a radical concept: a no-phone zone where violators face public humiliation by designated 'Shame Officers.' When a customer is caught using their phone, the Shame Officer will stand next to their table and announce, 'Attention: table 7 has chosen their screen over us. Let's give them a slow clap to acknowledge their decision.' Other customers are encouraged to stare disapprovingly while the violator's food gets cold from the extended attention. The cafe has already become a viral sensation, with one customer saying, 'I only checked my phone for 5 seconds and suddenly 40 strangers were booing me. I've never felt so seen.' The restaurant has since expanded to offer 'Phone Amnesty Days' where phones are allowed and customers cry with joy.
imagePrompt: Waiter standing next to customer with phone, holding sign saying SHAME, other customers staring, comic illustration, flat design, red and white colors
